title: Save 70x Tokens On Claude With This Plugin

eval.summary: Charlie Automates promotes a Claude Code plugin called Graphify, inspired by Andrej Karpathy's 'raw folder' strategy where all files are dropped into one directory for Claude to search. Graphify builds a knowledge graph of files and preemptively creates semantic relationships between them, so instead of Claude reading all files on every search, a 'Graphify query' command returns enriched, relationship-aware results. Claims 70x token reduction per search vs. naive full-file scanning. 39k GitHub stars after a few weeks. Output can be visualized in Obsidian. No deep technical implementation shown — primarily a promotional short teasing a fuller breakdown.

patterns.description: Pre-compute a knowledge graph with semantic relationships between files so agents query the graph rather than scanning all files — 'index once, query cheap' pattern
patterns.apply_to: How orchestrator.py and Claude access .lux/Data/ JSON files and session/working memory — instead of passing all files as context, build a lightweight index or manifest with relationship metadata that Claude queries first

patterns.description: Karpathy's raw folder strategy: designate one drop zone for all unstructured inputs; let the agent index and structure them rather than pre-organizing manually
patterns.apply_to: Inbound data handling in PersonalOS and TradeOS — intake folder pattern before processing into structured memory layers

patterns.description: Add a graph-query tool or manifest file to CLAUDE.md that tells Claude 'query the index first, then fetch specific files' rather than reading all context files upfront
patterns.apply_to: CLAUDE.md conventions — add an explicit instruction pattern: 'before reading files, check the index manifest at .lux/index.json to find relevant files and relationships'

patterns.description: Pre-compute file relationship metadata (which files depend on or reference which) and store as structured JSON so agents can navigate the codebase semantically
patterns.apply_to: CLAUDE.md or repo-level index file — a dependency/relationship map for alan_os_server.py, orchestrator.py, and supporting modules so Claude doesn't re-read unchanged files

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: Complements existing .lux/Data/*.json memory architecture — Graphify or its pattern could replace naive full-context file reads with graph-indexed lookups, sitting between orchestrator.py and Claude's context window
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Lens 1 drives this verdict: the 70x token reduction claim is unverified but the underlying pattern — precompute a relationship index, query it instead of scanning all files — is immediately applicable to how we manage memory file access in the current stack. Lens 2 is uncertain because Graphify is Claude Code specific (MCP/plugin layer) and may not integrate cleanly with our FastAPI+orchestrator architecture. Worth one test session to verify token savings and check if the graph-build step can run headlessly.
action_items: Check Graphify GitHub repo (search 'Graphify Claude Code knowledge graph') to verify API-first / headless operation before Claude Code dependency assessment

Immediately implement a lightweight version: create .lux/index.json manifest that maps each memory file to its purpose, related files, and last-modified — add CLAUDE.md instruction to check index before reading files
Benchmark current token usage on a representative orchestrator.py session with and without full .lux/Data context loading to establish baseline before evaluating any graph solution

strategic_signal.signal: Token cost at context-window scale is becoming a primary pain point for power Claude users — tools that reduce context load via pre-indexed relationships are gaining rapid traction (39k stars in weeks), signaling that 'smart context retrieval' is a near-term differentiator for any AgentOS product competing on efficiency.

eval.fit_rationale: Lens 1 drives this verdict: the 70x token reduction claim is unverified but the underlying pattern — precompute a relationship index, query it instead of scanning all files — is immediately applicable to how we manage memory file access in the current stack. Lens 2 is uncertain because Graphify is Claude Code specific (MCP/plugin layer) and may not integrate cleanly with our FastAPI+orchestrator architecture. Worth one test session to verify token savings and check if the graph-build step can run headlessly.
